titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Add. Ch. 76748

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=676 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:48:47.27971+00 | translation moa=677 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:48:49.231109+00

p.cl: Omnibus has literas visuris uel audituris Alwinus filius Duncani de fofrekin Salutem

me dedisse et concessisse et hac
carta mea confirmasse Malbrido Marrenah et heredibus suis terram illam que fuit in lite inter me et dominum laurentium filium orm
per rectas diuisas ab illi mec ewin usque ad modlahin et ex alia parte inter Gahnakarbet et aldi nalar ad tenendum
de me et heredibus meis ille et heredes sui libere et quiete ab omni seruicio et consuetudine et exactione et forinseco et ab aliquo
ausilio . et predictus Malbrid habebit comunam pasturam et multuram suam liberam eundo ubicumque . uoluerit et predictus Malbrid
habebit merchet et blodwit et vnlah libere quantum ad terram illam pertinet . et predictus malbrid reddet annuatim
et heredes sui mihi et heredibus meis octo solidos argenti . quatuor solidos . ad pentecosten et quatuor solidos ad festum sancti Martini

Testibus Waldeuo filio merleswain et herede suo . Duncano filio ade et herede suo Willelmo persona de Kilconcat . Toma de
lundin et herede suo . Malcolmo Iudice . et Multis aliis .
To all intending to view or hear these letters, Alwin son of Duncan of Foffarty [sends] greeting.

Know me to have given, conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to Maelbrigte Marrenah and his heirs that land which is in dispute between me and the lord Laurence son of Orm, by the right marches, from 'Illi mec Ewin' to 'Modlahin' and, from the other side, between 'Gahnakarbet' and 'Aldi Nalar'; to be held of me and my heirs by him and his heirs free and quit from all service, custom, exaction, forinsec, and from any aid; and the aforesaid Maelbrigte shall have his own free common pasture and multure going wherever he wishes, and the aforesaid Maelbrigte will have merchet, blodwite, and unlaw, freely, as much as pertains to that land; and the aforesaid Maelbrigte and his heirs shall annually render to me and my heirs eight shillings of silver, four shillings at Pentecost and four shillings at Martinmas.
As witnesses: Waldef son of Merlswain, and his heir; Duncan son of Adam, and his heir; William the parson of Kilconquhar; Thomas of Lundin, and his heir; Malcolm the judex; and many others.
